exemplar.title: How can the modeling of a sinusoidal function accurately predict the seasonal rainfall patterns in Bahrain using the historical data from 2014-2019?

detailedExemplarData.title: How can the modeling of a sinusoidal function accurately predict the seasonal rainfall patterns in Bahrain using the historical data from 2014-2019?

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

files.fileName: Hassan Moamen - Math AA Internal Assessment (3).pdf

strengths: The exploration is highly coherent with a clear introductionâmethodâanalysisâconclusion flow.
Sections are well signposted with numbered headings and transitions that guide the reader.
All material is relevant and concisely presented, with only very minor digressions.
weaknesses: Figure captions could be more detailed.
A few tables still split across pages awkwardly.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although there are minor formatting lapses (table breaks, minimal captions, citation style), the report is exceptionally well organized, logically developed and concise. This merits the top band (4).

strengths: Consistent and accurate use of trigonometric, statistical and modelling notation throughout.
Multiple representations (tables, bar charts, residual plots, Desmos graph) are effectively integrated and referenced.
Mathematical arguments are communicated clearly with very few implicit steps.
weaknesses: A handful of axis labels and units are still missing on some charts.
Minor stray symbols (âÃ·â, overlines) appear in a couple of equations.
Definitions of a few parameters (b, c, d) could be more explicitly stated.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While a few notational slips remain, the student uses appropriate mathematical language, multiple representations and logical structure consistently and accurately, warranting the highest band (4).

strengths: The choice of a personally collected data set shows genuine independent thinking.
Linking the sinusoidal model to a hobby (football cancellations) is clearly personal.
The residualâplot idea and use of match data demonstrate creativity beyond the minimum.
weaknesses: The modelling approach remains a standard syllabus technique rather than highly innovative.
Connections to broader contexts (e.g., climate science) are not deeply explored.
Some aspects of the personal journey and decisionâmaking process are underdeveloped.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student shows significant independent thinking, a clear personal approach and good creativity, but does not attain the outstanding, highly innovative level of the top band, so band 2 is appropriate.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on skewness, coefficient of variation and model limitations.
Thoughtful analysis of residual patterns and over-predictions in the dry season.
Clear evaluation of how the model predicts cancellations and suggestions for future improvements.
weaknesses: Reflection remains at a general level; deeper critique (e.g., piecewise models) is not developed.
No quantitative goodness-of-fit metric (RÂ² or RMSE) is reported to support the discussion.
Limited personal insight into the learning journey and challenges faced.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student provides meaningful and consistent reflection, analysis and evaluation, but the depth and critical insight fall short of the top band. Band 2 accurately reflects the level of reflection achieved.

strengths: Mathematical techniques (sinusoidal regression, standard deviation, residual analysis) are applied expertly throughout.
Calculations and graphs display a thorough understanding with correct parameter derivations.
All mathematics is fully commensurate with and expertly applied at SL level, serving the research question at every stage.
weaknesses: Minor rounding inconsistencies in a couple of calculations.
The treatment of zero rainfall periods could be better justified within the sinusoidal framework.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Despite small computational slips, the mathematics is used comprehensively and at a high level of sophistication. The exploration demonstrates expert application and complete correctness in almost all aspects, justifying the maximum mark of 6.

strandSelections.reasoning: Arguments proceed in a logical order and most steps are explained in sentences rather than left implicit.  Occasional algebra is compressed, and the connection between football cancellations and rainfall could be tighter, but overall clarity is good, meriting band 3.

Calculations and graphs are largely correct; parameter derivations are sound, and residuals are computed properly.  Minor rounding inconsistencies and the failure to address negative sine outputs indicate some imperfection, hence âgood understanding and mostly correctâ matches band 4.

The student employs tables, bar charts, an area graph, a Desmos plot and a residual scatterplotâmultiple forms that meaningfully support the discussion.  While effective, some graphics are low-resolution or lack labelled axes, so the use is âeffectiveâ rather than âhighly effective,â placing it in band 3.

The report follows a clear introductionâmethodâanalysisâconclusion sequence, and each section develops naturally from the previous one.  Transitional phrases explain why the next step is being taken (e.g., justification of the sine model, then parameter determination, then residual analysis).  Minor lapsesâsuch as occasional digressions into climate description and duplicated statistical explanationsâprevent the work from feeling âhighly coherent,â but overall the logic is easy to follow, warranting band 3 rather than 4.

Creating residual plots and introducing match-cancellation data demonstrate creativity beyond the minimum, but the modelling technique itself is conventional.  Hence a âgood levelâ of creativity (band 2) is shown.

There is thoughtful analysis of residuals and acknowledgment that the sinusoid over-predicts dry-season rainfall.  However, proposed refinements remain general; a deeper mathematical critique (e.g., piecewise or truncated sine) is lacking.  Band 2 is therefore appropriate.

The conclusion evaluates how well the model predicts cancellations and notes limitations and future improvements, satisfying âclear evaluation of outcomes.â  It stops short of a comprehensive treatment with quantitative measures such as RÂ², so band 2 is justified.

The student reflects on skewness, coefficient of variation, and model limitations (zero rainfall periods), showing meaningful reflection.  Discussion is accurate but not deeply critical or sustained, fitting band 2 for depth.

Variables, parameters and statistical symbols are generally defined and typeset correctly; trigonometric notation and units appear consistently.  A few slips (missing degree/month units, âÃ·,â stray overlines) show that usage is not flawless, so band 3 (mostly consistent) is appropriate rather than band 4.

Linking the model to the studentâs hobby provides a clear personal approach, and the investigation is guided by the studentâs own question.  The approach, however, is typical of sinusoidal-modelling IAs and not especially innovative, aligning with band 2.

Most content is relevant to modelling rainfall; however, lengthy restatements of background climate information, repeated mean calculations, and decorative symbols (\( \omega,\; \blacksquare \)) add unnecessary bulk.  At â4 000 words the exploration feels wordy, so it is âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elements,â matching band 2.

The IA applies relevant mathematicsâsinusoidal regression, basic statistics, residual analysisâdirectly to the research question, and each technique serves a purpose.  Coverage is effective but not yet comprehensive (only one functional form tried), so band 4 (effective) fits.

Choosing a personally collected football-cancellation data set and deriving model parameters by hand shows âsignificantâ but not âoutstandingâ independent thinking.  The mathematics remains standard syllabus material, so band 2 fits.

Headings, numbered sections, and tables give the exploration a clear structure; figures are referenced in the text and appear in a sensible order.  The reader can locate data, calculations and results quickly.  Nevertheless, some tables split awkwardly across pages and figure captions are minimal, so the organization is not âexceptionallyâ polished, just solidly âwell-organized.â

All mathematics is at or above SL level (trigonometric modelling, standard deviation) and used appropriately.  The level is fully commensurate with the course but not extended beyond it, so band 4 is appropriate.

annotations.comment: The mean calculation is repeated in several sections. Consider consolidating statistical derivations to reduce redundancy and improve conciseness.
Bullet about bimodal distribution is asserted without a dedicated graph or histogram. Including a specific representation would strengthen the argument.
Phase shift derivation selects n=0 without discussion of other integer solutions. Reflect on why the simplest solution is appropriate and its impact on fit.
The title is precise and clearly indicates the focus on sinusoidal modeling of rainfall in Bahrain, providing an effective introduction to the exploration.
The background description of Bahrainâs climate is informative but overly lengthy. Consider streamlining this section to maintain conciseness and focus on key details.
Only two parameters are defined in the list; definitions for b (frequency), c (phase shift) and d (vertical shift) are missing here. Include complete definitions for clarity.
Assumption that external climate factors are minimal lacks justification. Suggest including evidence or references to support the neglect of variability due to regional influences.
The sentence ends abruptly and seems incomplete (âwith over â¦â). Verify the text and ensure the final clause is fully captured.
Notation in the methodology list includes stray symbols (e.g., âÃ·,â âÏ_effâ) that are not explained. Standardize notation and remove extraneous symbols for clarity.
Provide more detail on how the football matchâcancellation data were collected and verified to strengthen the reliability of the primary data source.
Table 1 shows many months with zero rainfall. Discuss how these extended zero periods might suggest a piecewise or modified model rather than a simple sine function.
The bar chart lacks axis labels and units on both axes. Adding these would improve mathematical communication and reader comprehension.
Units (mm) are omitted in the mean formula. Consistently include units in equations to maintain precision in mathematical communication.
The median calculation lists each zero individually. Group repeated values or use frequency notation to make the presentation more concise.
The standard deviation formula uses nâ1 but the computation divides by 12 rather than 11, indicating a misapplication of the sample formula. Correct this for accuracy.
When presenting the coefficient of variation, comment on its implication for model reliability and how it informs the need for a cyclical model.
Large negative residuals during summer highlight model overprediction in dry months. Discuss how a modified function or piecewise fit could address this systematic error.
When defining R(t) globally, specify that t corresponds to month number and clarify domain (e.g., tâ{1,2,â¦,12}) to avoid ambiguity.
Figure 3 is introduced as a section header rather than a caption. Use consistent caption formatting and reference the model equation directly in the graph.
Notation in the parameter list includes unexplained symbols (e.g., âÏ_c,â âL.â). Replace with clear labels a, b, c, d and include units where relevant.
Acknowledgement of negative sine values is good; follow up with a quantitative proposal (e.g., truncated model) rather than a general statement for deeper reflection.
Cancellationârate table uses mixed notation (bold, overline) inconsistently. Adopt a single, clear notation for counts to improve readability.
Instead of showing only Januaryâs prediction, present a concise table of predicted values for all months to give a comprehensive view of model performance.
Residual example is clear, but include a statistical goodnessâofâfit metric (e.g., RÂ² or RMSE) to quantitatively assess model accuracy.
Discussion of residual patterns would be strengthened by reporting RMSE or mean absolute error. This quantifies the fit rather than relying solely on visual interpretations.
The conclusion effectively summarizes findings but offers limited insight into the studentâs learning journey. Add a personal reflection on challenges and skills gained.
The limitations section identifies general constraints but could propose specific mathematical refinementsâsuch as introducing a truncated sine or logistic transitionâto deepen critical analysis.
References are inconsistently formatted (URLs on separate lines, variable citation styles). Adopt a standard citation style throughout to improve presentation.
